metadata.working_title: Design Principles for Inflammasome Inhibition by Pyrin-Only-Proteins

abstract_sketch.problem: Inflammasomes are filamentous signaling platforms essential for innate host defense, but dysregulated assembly contributes to autoinflammatory disorders, cancer, and severe COVID-19. Endogenous pyrin-only-proteins (POPs) have emerged as key inhibitors, yet their intrinsic target specificities and inhibition mechanisms remain speculative.
abstract_sketch.gap: Prior conclusions about POP specificity were inferred from indirect in cellulo and in vivo measurements that harbor multiple inflammasome components simultaneously; the direct protein-protein recognition and filament-assembly inhibition mechanisms have not been defined, and the widespread assumption that amino-acid sequence homology predicts POP targets has not been rigorously tested.
abstract_sketch.approach: We combined Rosetta in silico interface energy analyses on honeycomb-like filament sideviews of ASC, AIM2, IFI16, NLRP3, and NLRP6 PYDs with in cellulo HEK293T co-transfection imaging (mCherry-PYD + eGFP-POP) and in vitro FRET-based polymerization kinetics and negative-stain electron microscopy of purified recombinant POPs.
abstract_sketch.key_results: POP1 is a poor inhibitor of ASC (less than or equal to 20 percent suppression of ASCPYD filaments) but suppresses assembly of upstream receptor PYDs including AIM2, IFI16, NLRP3, and NLRP6 (approximately 60 to 70 percent suppression at 1200 ng). POP2 and POP3 strongly impede the nucleation of ASCPYD (up to approximately 15 min lag extension) and also interfere with elongation of AIM2PYD and NLRP6PYD filaments. A combination of favorable recognition interfaces and strongly unfavorable repulsion interfaces is required for POPs to block PYD filament assembly.
abstract_sketch.impact: Target specificity of POPs is not dictated by sequence homology but by a degenerate-code-like recognition mechanism in which POPs act as decoy adaptors or receptors. This redefines the regulatory logic of inflammasome inhibition and provides design principles for therapeutic modulation of persistent inflammasome activity.

sections.title: Introduction
content_bullets: Inflammasomes are filamentous innate-immune signaling platforms that respond to pathogen invasion, genotoxic stress, and ionizing radiation
Persistent inflammasome activity causes autoinflammatory disease, cancer, and severe COVID-19, motivating mechanistic dissection of their regulators
Inflammasome signaling proceeds by sequential PYD-PYD filament assembly: receptor PYD -> ASCPYD -> ASCCARD -> caspase-1 -> IL-1beta and pyroptosis
Inflammasome assembly is binary (on-or-off) and prion-like; positive feedback makes it irreversible, demanding extrinsic regulators

Mammalian pyrin-only-proteins (POPs) are 100-amino-acid six-helix bundles analogous to COPs; POP1, POP2, POP3 are proposed to inhibit inflammasomes by sequence homology: POP1~ASCPYD (65%), POP2~NLRP2PYD (68%), POP3~AIM2PYD (67%)

Prior models based on homology and indirect assays predict discrete targets, but the direct mechanism has not been tested and receptor PYDs lack pairwise homology yet all signal through ASCPYD, suggesting degenerate-code recognition
We combine Rosetta in silico interface energy analysis, in cellulo imaging in HEK293T cells, and in vitro FRET polymerization plus nsEM to delineate POP target specificity and mechanism of inhibition

subsections.title: Rosetta interface analyses suggest broad target specificities of POPs
content_bullets: We adopted a Rosetta honeycomb strategy in which the central protomer makes all six Type 1a/b, 2a/b, 3a/b contacts and interface energies (DeltaG) are computed for homotypic PYD-PYD and POP-PYD assemblies
Inflammasome components chosen for tractability and biological importance: ASC, AIM2, IFI16, NLRP3, NLRP6
Homotypic PYD-PYD interactions are symmetric (Type 1a and 1b equal; e.g., ASCPYD-ASCPYD DeltaG = -22.1), confirming bidirectional filament growth
Homotypic DeltaGs are always more favorable than any POP-PYD interaction (e.g., ASCPYD-ASCPYD top-half sum = -35.9 vs POP1-ASCPYD = -24.7), implying excess POPs are required for inhibition
POP1 shows the most favorable top-half interface with ASCPYD but POP3 matches it at the bottom (POP1-ASCPYD = -34.3 vs POP3-ASCPYD = -34.6)

For AIM2PYD all three POPs are comparable on the bottom half; POP3 is most favorable for IFI16PYD (top half DeltaG = -9.6), yet POP1 and POP2 still exceed homotypic IFI16-IFI16 at individual interfaces
For NLRP3PYD, POP1 is nearly as favorable as homotypic contacts (POP1-NLRP3PYD = -30.5 vs NLRP3PYD-NLRP3PYD = -31.4); POP2/3 interactions are much less favorable
Analyses rationalize some proposed specificities but do not explain why POP1 is predicted to bind ASC best while in vivo evidence favors POP2; motivates biochemical follow-up

subsections.title: Mechanisms of ASC inhibition by POPs
content_bullets: HEK293T cells lack endogenous inflammasome components and POPs, giving a clean co-transfection system
ASCPYD-mCherry (300 ng) forms filaments and ASCFL-mCherry forms puncta when co-transfected with POP-eGFP (600 or 1200 ng) or eGFP alone
POP1-eGFP minimally inhibits ASCPYD filaments (less than or equal to 20 percent) while POP2 and POP3 strongly reduce them (more diffuse mCherry, fewer linear filaments)
ASCFL puncta: POP1 reduces by approximately 30 percent, POP2/3 up to approximately 80 percent
Recombinant POP1 is monomeric while POP2 and POP3 require an N-terminal MBP tag and form elongated oligomers after TEVp cleavage (nsEM)
FRET polymerization of ASCPYD (2.5 microM, donor:acceptor 1:1) triggered by TEVp cleavage of MBP shows two phases: rate-limiting nucleation (lag) and elongation
POP1 up to 150 microM does not affect ASCPYD polymerization kinetics or nsEM filament formation
POP2 (3.3, 6.7, 13.3, 26.7, 40 microM) and POP3 (3.25, 7.5, 15, 20, 30 microM) prolong the lag up to approximately 15 min and reduce the elongation slope by less than or equal to 20 percent
nsEM: no ASCPYD filaments in the presence of POP2; only a few with POP3, confirming nucleation block rather than reversible inhibition
POP2 and POP3 impede nucleation of ASCPYD; POP1 is only marginally effective, contradicting the classical POP1-as-ASC-inhibitor model

subsections.title: Re-examining the Rosetta analyses in light of biochemical experiments
content_bullets: Interface profiles differ: POP2/3-ASCPYD contain multiple strongly unfavorable interfaces (DeltaDeltaG >= 10.0; ASCPYD-ASCPYD minus POP-PYD) while POP1-ASCPYD lacks such repulsion
All three POPs contain favorable recognition surfaces for ASCPYD (DeltaDeltaG <= 3.5)
Hypothesis: a combination of favorable (recognition) and strongly unfavorable (repulsion) interfaces is necessary for POPs to inhibit PYD filament assembly

subsections.title: Mechanisms of ALR inhibition by POPs
content_bullets: AIM2PYD and IFI16PYD show a mixture of favorable and unfavorable interfaces with all three POPs in Rosetta, consistent with the amended recognition hypothesis
HEK293T imaging: POP1-eGFP reduces AIM2PYD and IFI16PYD filaments approximately 60 percent at 1200 ng, compared with approximately 20 percent for ASCPYD
POP2 and POP3 essentially eliminate AIM2PYD and IFI16PYD filaments in cells

FRET kinetics of AIM2PYD (2.5 microM) with POP1 (25, 50, 100, 150 microM), POP2 (12.5, 25, 50, 75 microM), POP3 (7.5, 15, 30, 40, 50 microM): all three POPs reduce elongation slope dose-dependently with minimal lag change; POP3 is most effective (lowest IC50)
nsEM: POP1 makes AIM2PYD filaments shorter and fewer; POP2/3 abolish filament formation
Full-length AIM2 dsDNA binding: POP1 fails to interfere, POP2 and POP3 inhibit; for IFI16FL only POP3 is inhibitory
All three POPs can interfere with ALR filament assembly (POP3 strongest); activating ligand (dsDNA) diminishes the inhibitory effect

subsections.title: POP1 likely targets upstream receptors instead of ASC
content_bullets: Hypothesis: POP1 is a decoy adaptor that interferes with upstream receptors rather than directly inhibiting ASC

Rosetta: POP1 shows favorable and unfavorable interfaces with NLRP3PYD and NLRP6PYD; POP2/3 show largely unfavorable DeltaGs but retain at least one interface (Type 3a, DeltaG ~ -9) that can mediate recognition
NLRP2PYD-mCherry failed to form filaments in HEK293T cells, precluding POP2 testing despite high sequence similarity
HEK293T imaging: at 1200 ng, POP1 suppresses NLRP3PYD by 70 percent and NLRP6PYD by 60 percent, compared with approximately 20 percent for ASCPYD
At 600 ng POP2, ASCPYD assembly is abolished but NLRP3PYD and NLRP6PYD are barely suppressed
At 600 ng POP3, AIM2PYD is suppressed 70 percent while ASCPYD, NLRP3PYD, and NLRP6PYD are reduced 40 to 50 percent

FRET kinetics of NLRP6PYD (2.5 microM) with POP1 (25, 50, 100 microM), POP2 (30, 60, 120 microM), POP3 (15, 30, 60 microM): POP1 predominantly inhibits elongation, POP2 inhibits nucleation, POP3 reduces both
nsEM: POP1/2/3 reduce the number and length of NLRP6PYD filaments; oligomers form (FRET rises) but do not mature into intact filaments
POP1 acts as a decoy ASC that interferes with upstream PYDs; favorable plus unfavorable interfaces are necessary to block PYD filament assembly

subsections.title: Redefining the target specificity and inhibitory mechanisms of POPs
content_bullets: Inflammasomes are exceptionally stable and prion-like; their aberrant activity underlies autoimmune disease and severe COVID-19, so early-stage regulation by POPs is biologically critical
POPs interfere with polymerization without co-assembling, unlike COPs that co-assemble with caspase-1 CARD
Excess POPs are required to inhibit inflammasome PYDs (especially with activating ligands such as dsDNA), consistent with Rosetta predicting homotypic PYD-PYD interactions as the most favorable
POPs can modulate both nucleation and elongation, giving versatile regulation without shutting down innate immunity

subsections.title: Revised role of POP1 as a decoy ASC
content_bullets: POP1 is minimally effective against ASCPYD but interferes with AIM2, IFI16, NLRP3, NLRP6 filament assembly, particularly at the elongation step
POP1 expression is induced by IL-1beta and thus likely operates as a negative-feedback regulator that stabilizes rather than terminates inflammasome signaling
Figure 5 summarizes revised target selection: POP1 acts as a decoy adaptor for receptor PYDs

subsections.title: POP2 and POP3 as dual-target inhibitors
content_bullets: POP2 potently suppresses nucleation of ASCPYD and also inhibits AIM2PYD, IFI16PYD, and NLRPs; induced by both pro- and anti-inflammatory cytokines, acting as a pan-inflammasome inhibitor
POP3 inhibits elongation of AIM2PYD, can act on NLRPs, and suppresses ASCPYD nucleation; induced exclusively by interferons and thus ALR-biased in physiological context
Sequence homology does not predict target specificity; each POP has intrinsic affinity for multiple inflammasome components

subsections.title: Design principles and the favorable-plus-unfavorable-interface rule
content_bullets: POP1-ASCPYD lacks strongly unfavorable interfaces and fails to inhibit polymerization; POP1-NLRP3PYD has only one strongly unfavorable interface yet is inhibitory
POP2/3-NLRP6PYD lack strongly favorable interactions but one interface (Type 3a) with DeltaG ~ -9 is sufficient to recognize and disrupt assembly
All six protomer interfaces are needed for filament assembly; POPs need only one favorable recognition surface plus at least one unfavorable surface to disrupt assembly
Nonequilibrium assembly kinetics favor homotypic interactions unless POPs supply strongly unfavorable interfaces or multimeric steric interference (relevant to multimeric POP2/3)
POPs may preferentially trap pseudo-nucleation intermediates, consistent with FRET signal rising while nsEM shows no mature filaments

subsections.title: Implications, limitations, and outlook
content_bullets: Degenerate-code-like recognition extends from PYD-PYD signaling onto POP-PYD regulation, explaining how a small number of POPs can regulate diverse receptors
Future work should employ molecular dynamics and extensive mutagenesis at the favorable-plus-unfavorable interface to test causality
The framework provides design principles for rationally engineered POP variants or small-molecule mimics that could modulate specific inflammasomes therapeutically
Limitations include inability to assay NLRP2PYD (no filament formation in HEK293T), nuclear localization of IFI16FL preventing direct co-imaging, and IC50 ranking reflects in vitro conditions only

sections.title: Materials and Methods
content_bullets: Rosetta InterfaceAnalyzer on honeycomb sideviews using cryo-EM structures: ASCPYD (PDB 3J63), AIM2PYD (PDB 7K3R), NLRP3PYD (PDB 7PDZ), NLRP6PYD (PDB 6NCV); IFI16PYD modeled on eGFP-AIM2 (PDB 6MB2); POP1 (PDB 4QOB); POP2 and POP3 homology modeled on NLRP3PYD (7PDZ) and AIM2PYD (7K3R)

Cell culture: HEK293T (ATCC CRL-11268), 12-well plate, 0.1 x 10^6 cells/well, 70 percent confluence, lipofectamine 2000; PYD-mCherry 300 or 600 ng + POP-eGFP or eGFP 600 or 1200 ng; 16 h, 1x PBS wash, 4 percent paraformaldehyde, Cytation 5 imaging, Gen5 analysis

Recombinant proteins: POP1 in pET21b, POP2/POP3 in pET28b with N-terminal His6-MBP + TEVp site; expressed in E. coli BL21 Rosetta2(DE3); Ni2+-NTA + SEC in 40 mM HEPES-NaOH pH 7.4, 400 mM NaCl, 2 mM DTT, 0.5 mM EDTA, 10 percent glycerol; stored at -80 degrees Celsius
FRET polymerization: 1:1 donor-acceptor labeled MBP-PYD; polymerization triggered by TEVp; t1/2 and IC50 (50 percent decrease in 1/t1/2) computed as in prior work; n = 3
FA-based dsDNA binding assays for AIM2FL and IFI16FL with increasing POPs; MBP pre-cleaved by TEVp for 30 min
nsEM: TEVp-cleaved MBP-PYDs polymerized with or without POPs; carbon-coated grids imaged as previously described

table_plan.title: Rosetta interface energies (DeltaG, Rosetta Energy Units) for homotypic PYD-PYD assemblies and POP-PYD interactions
table_plan.caption_sketch: Interface energies for ASC, AIM2, IFI16, NLRP3, NLRP6 PYDs and their putative interactions with POP1, POP2, POP3; homotypic DeltaGs are always more favorable than POP-PYD DeltaGs.

table_plan.title: Cellular suppression of PYD filament and puncta formation by POPs in HEK293T cells
table_plan.caption_sketch: Percent reduction in ASCPYD, ASCFL, AIM2PYD, IFI16PYD, NLRP3PYD, NLRP6PYD filaments/puncta with 1200 ng POP-eGFP relative to eGFP alone; values taken from cellular imaging experiments with n >= 4.

table_plan.title: Kinetic regime of PYD polymerization inhibition by POPs in FRET assays
table_plan.caption_sketch: Qualitative nucleation and elongation effects of each POP on ASCPYD, AIM2PYD, and NLRP6PYD polymerization. POP1 did not affect ASCPYD up to 150 microM; POP2/3 extended ASCPYD lag by up to approximately 15 min and reduced elongation slope by <= 20 percent.
